project.name: Meridian Integration
project.description: All engineering and compliance work required to close the Meridian Health Systems deal. Target: March 15, 2026. $2,800/mo Enterprise. P0 — revenue critical.

issues.title: Implement per-org retention configuration
issues.description:
## Context
Meridian requires 1-year raw event retention. Our current retention policy is global — 90 days for raw events, 2 years for hourly aggregates. We need per-org overrides so Enterprise customers can have custom retention without affecting the default for Growth tier.

## What needs to happen
- Add `retention_days` field to org settings table in PostgreSQL
- ClickHouse TTL policy needs to respect per-org config rather than global setting
- Admin UI to configure retention per org (internal-only for now — not customer-facing)
- Data backfill: Meridian's data should be retained from contract start date

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Org settings API supports `raw_retention_days` and `aggregate_retention_days` fields
- [ ] ClickHouse partition TTL respects per-org config
- [ ] Default behavior unchanged for non-Enterprise orgs
- [ ] Covered by integration tests
- [ ] Documented in internal runbook

## Notes
See architecture-spec-v2.md in Drive for ClickHouse schema context. This blocks the Meridian SOW — do not let it slip past March 1.

Related: VEC-52 (Clerk migration) should land first — auth touches the org settings layer.

comments.body: This needs to be configurable at the org level, not global. The ClickHouse TTL is currently set as a table-level policy — we'll need to move to partition-level TTL expressions keyed on org_id. Liam, worth reading the ClickHouse docs on `TTL expr + TO DISK` before scoping — the naive approach here will hurt query performance.

read it. partition-level TTL is the right call. one complication: ClickHouse doesn't support row-level TTL on the MergeTree engine without a separate materialized view per org — which doesn't scale. better approach is a background job that runs nightly and deletes rows past the org's retention threshold. less elegant but it works.

will write up the approach before touching code.
this is blocking the Meridian deal, prioritize

issues.title: Migrate auth from Firebase to Clerk (SAML SSO support)
issues.description:
## Context
Firebase Auth doesn't support org-level SAML. Meridian requires SAML SSO before they'll sign. Clerk is the chosen replacement — supports SAML and OIDC natively, has a good Go SDK, and integrates cleanly with our Next.js frontend.

Marcus's comparison doc: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/vectrix/auth-provider-comparison.md

## Scope
### Backend (Liam)
- Replace Firebase token validation middleware with Clerk JWT verification
- Update org creation flow to support Clerk org IDs
- Handle Firebase token fallback for existing users during migration window
- Session management: move from Firebase session cookies to Clerk sessions

### Frontend (Sofia)
- Replace Firebase Auth SDK with Clerk React components
- Update login, signup, and org invite flows
- Add SAML SSO configuration UI for Enterprise orgs
- Test across Chrome, Firefox, Safari

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All new signups use Clerk
- [ ] Existing Firebase users migrated without re-login required
- [ ] SAML SSO flow tested end-to-end with a test IdP (Okta dev account)
- [ ] Firebase Auth dependency removed from codebase
- [ ] Zero auth-related errors in Sentry for 48hrs post-cutover

## Risk
This touches every authenticated route. Ship incrementally. Do not merge the Firebase removal until all existing customers are confirmed migrated.

comments.body:
frontend side is ~80% done. login + signup flows are on Clerk, invite flow is the last piece. found a weird edge case where users with existing Firebase sessions get a CORS error on the token exchange endpoint — @liam is this on your side or mine?

![clerk-cors-error](https://linear-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/placeholder/clerk-cors-error-screenshot.png)
yours. the token exchange endpoint allows clerk.vectrix.io origin but not app.vectrix.io — CORS config in `internal/middleware/cors.go` line 34. one-line fix.

status check: where are we on the 6 existing customer migrations? Meridian can't be the first — we need at least one Enterprise customer (Cascade) successfully migrated on Clerk before we enable SSO for Meridian.
4 of 6 migrated. Cascade and Brainloop still on Firebase. Cascade is priority — I'll do their migration manually this week with Jordan on standby. Brainloop can follow.

issues.title: Complete Meridian security questionnaire responses
issues.description:
## Context
Meridian sent their standard vendor security assessment on Jan 20. 147 questions across 9 categories. Rachel Simmons (Security & Compliance Lead) is the point of contact. Responses due Jan 31 (10 business days).

## Ownership by section
| Section | Owner | Status |
|---|---|---|
| 1 - Data Security | Nina | In Progress |
| 2 - Access Control | Marcus | In Progress |
| 3 - Incident Response | Nina + Jordan | In Progress |
| 4 - Compliance | Nina (Priya review) | In Progress |
| 5 - Business Continuity | Nina | Not started |
| 6 - Data Residency | Marcus | Done |
| 7 - Vendor Management | Nina | Not started |
| 8 - Encryption | Marcus | Not started |
| 9 - Physical Security | Nina | Not started |

## Notes
- SOC 2 Type I report is in Drive — Nina to attach
- Question 38 (data breach notification policy) — Nina to draft from scratch, no existing doc
- SAML SSO status: note as 'in progress, target Feb 28'
- Do not send until Priya has reviewed Section 4 in full

comments.body:
Shared doc is live in Drive for tracking responses. Jordan — I need your input on Section 3 (Incident Response), specifically questions 41-47. They're asking about our on-call structure, escalation path, and P1 response SLAs. Can you fill in what you know and I'll clean it up?

Also flagging: question 52 asks whether we have a formal DR (disaster recovery) plan document. Do we? I couldn't find one in Drive.
on it for 41-47. DR plan: we don't have a formal doc. the actual DR setup is cross-region S3 backups + EKS failover config but nobody wrote it up. I'll draft something this week.

issues.title: Draft and send Meridian SOW for legal review
issues.description:
## Context
Meridian sent a draft SOW on Feb 10. Terms:
- $2,800/mo Enterprise, 12-month term
- 99.9% uptime SLA with financial credits
- Custom 1-year raw retention
- Data residency: US-only
- SOC 2 Type II required before contract execution (interim: Type I + commitment letter)
- Termination: 60-day written notice. Immediate termination for material breach or SLA failure >3 consecutive months.

James Hartley at Orion Legal is reviewing. Priya and Nina are internal reviewers.

## Status
Orion Legal has the draft. Expecting redlines back by Feb 17.

## Open items
- [ ] Legal redlines incorporated
- [ ] Priya sign-off
- [ ] SOC 2 Type II commitment letter drafted (Nina)
- [ ] Countersigned copy sent to Rachel Simmons

issues.title: Meridian demo environment setup
issues.description:
stand up a isolated demo org in prod for Alan's team to evaluate. needs to be pre-populated with realistic synthetic API traffic so the dashboard isn't empty on the call.

jordan to provision, liam to write the traffic gen script

comments.body:
demo org is live. org_id: `meridian-demo-01`. provisioned with 30 days of synthetic traffic across 12 endpoints. Liam's script is in `scripts/traffic-gen/` — README explains the parameters.

dashboard looks good. anomaly detection fired correctly on the synthetic latency spike we injected. marking done.
